topic: A student preparing for an exam
text: I balanced the pencil on the tip of my nose, tilting my head back until the yellow wood teetered at a precarious angle. The complex Fourier transforms on the page could wait another five minutes while I attempted to beat my personal record. I let out a soft snort, sending the pencil tumbling onto my textbook, and I immediately tried to catch it between my index fingers like a falling baton. With a wink at my reflection in the darkened window, I began drumming a rhythmic salsa beat against the desk with two highlighters.

topic: A student preparing for an exam
text: Leo turned the flashcards into a high-stakes game of solitaire, flicking the ones he answered correctly across the rug with a triumphant 'swish' sound. When he encountered a particularly dense definition of neoclassical economics, he read it aloud in a thick, exaggerated aristocratic accent, gesturing grandly at his desk lamp as if it were a member of parliament. He took a sip of his juice box, blowing bubbles into the straw until the crinkle of the plastic made him giggle, then performed a theatrical bow to his empty chair.

topic: A student preparing for an exam
text: The margin of my notebook was no longer reserved for annotations; it was now the site of an epic battle between a doodle of a fire-breathing dragon and a very confused-looking isosceles triangle. I stuck my tongue out in concentration, adding tiny scales to the dragon’s tail. Every time I memorized a line of poetry, I rewarded myself by tossing a gummy bear into the air and trying to catch it in my mouth. On the fourth attempt, it bounced off my forehead, and I collapsed into a fit of silent, shoulder-shaking laughter.

topic: A student preparing for an exam
text: Maya spun around in her swivel chair, the library's fluorescent lights blurring into a dizzying halo. She stopped herself with a sudden planted foot and immediately began using her ruler as a pretend violin bow, 'playing' a frantic concerto over her chemistry notes. She spotted a friend three tables away and sent a silent, exaggeratedly dramatic wink their way, followed by a series of rapid-fire hand signals that suggested she was currently communicating in a top-secret spy cipher rather than studying.

topic: A student preparing for an exam
text: I had fashioned a small basketball hoop out of a wire coat hanger and hooked it over the top of my monitor. After successfully reciting the stages of mitosis, I crumpled a scrap piece of paper into a tight ball and took a 'three-pointer' from across the room. 'The crowd goes wild!' I whispered, miming a standing ovation from a stadium full of fans. I did a little victory shimmy in my seat, adjusted my glasses with a flourish, and dove back into the biology text with a wide, mischievous grin.

topic: A chef cooking a meal for guests
text: I tossed a handful of flour into the air, watching the white mist settle over the counter like a dusting of fresh snow. With a mischievous glint in my eye, I used my index finger to trace a caricature of the head waiter in the powder. As the pasta water began to bubble, I conducted an invisible orchestra with my whisk, punctuating each crescendo with a rhythmic tap against the copper pot. I caught a glimpse of my reflection in the polished fridge door and gave myself a quick, jaunty wink before sliding a tray of appetizers into the oven with a theatrical flourish.

topic: A chef cooking a meal for guests
text: The kitchen echoed with the sound of Julian humming a bright, uptempo jazz tune as he julienned carrots with rhythmic precision. He paused to balance a cherry tomato on his chin, grinning when his sous-chef looked over in mock exasperation. When it came time to deglaze the pan, he tilted the skillet just enough to send a controlled burst of flame upward, laughing softly as he did a tiny victory two-step away from the stove. He sampled the sauce, smacked his lips loudly, and gave a thumbs-up to the wall clock as if it were a judge on a cooking show.

topic: A chef cooking a meal for guests
text: I had turned the plating station into a canvas, using the balsamic glaze to create intricate, swirling spirals that danced around the seared scallops. I reached for a sprig of mint and tucked it behind my ear, grinning at the absurdity of my reflection in the soup tureen. When the bell rang for service, I did a quick spin on my heel, presenting the finished plates to the server with a dramatic, low bow and a whispered, 'Prepare for a taste-bud revolution, my friend.' My shoulders bounced with a suppressed chuckle as I watched them hurry away.

topic: A chef cooking a meal for guests
text: Sophie whistled a birdcall as she piped delicate rosettes of whipped cream onto the tartlets, occasionally stopping to 'boop' a tiny dot of cream onto the tip of her own nose. She moved through the kitchen with a light, springy step, sidestepping the prep table as if she were navigating a dance floor. While the soufflés rose, she took two wooden spoons and played a quick, clattering drum solo on the edge of the sink, her eyes sparkling with a bright, infectious energy. She blew a kiss toward the dining room door, whispering a secret joke to the rising dough.

topic: A chef cooking a meal for guests
text: I decided the garnishes needed a bit more 'personality,' so I carved the radishes into tiny, blooming tulips with wide, petal-smiles. As I waited for the roast to rest, I practiced juggling three limes, my eyes wide and focused as they looped through the air in a green blur. One lime escaped and bounced off the cutting board, and I lunged for it with the agility of a goalkeeper, coming up with a triumphant 'Aha!' I tucked the fruit back into the bowl, smoothed my apron, and performed a silent, seated jig while the timer counted down the final thirty seconds.

topic: A parent watching their child play
text: I crouched behind the thick trunk of the oak tree, peeking out with one eye as Leo searched the bushes. When he turned his back, I let out a low, exaggerated 'rawr' and ducked back into the shadows, stifling a giggle with the palm of my hand. I watched his little head spin around in confusion, his cape fluttering behind him. As he began to creep toward my hiding spot, I grabbed a handful of fallen leaves and prepared to launch a gentle golden shower over his head the moment he rounded the corner.

topic: A parent watching their child play
text: Every time Maya zoomed past on her tricycle, David made a loud, high-pitched 'neee-um' sound like a Formula One race car. He held up an invisible checkered flag and did a silly little shimmy as she crossed the 'finish line' marked by a stick in the dirt. When she stopped to inspect a ladybug, he crept up behind her on tiptoe, making dramatic, silent-movie gestures to an imaginary audience. He waited for her to look up before making a funny face, crossing his eyes and sticking out his tongue until she collapsed into heaps of laughter.

topic: A parent watching their child play
text: I sat on the edge of the sandbox, using my fingers to march a plastic dinosaur across the dunes toward my daughter’s sandcastle. 'Halt! Who goes there?' I squeaked in a comical, gravelly voice, making the T-Rex do a clumsy backflip into the moat. She squealed and started building a wall to block my path, so I began to hum a dramatic mission-impossible theme song. I winked at her, then made the dinosaur 'nibble' on her toes, prompting a flurry of giggles that made my own shoulders shake with silent mirth.

topic: A parent watching their child play
text: The bubbles floated through the air in iridescent clusters, and Sarah danced among them, popping them with her nose to mimic her toddler’s movements. She did a theatrical stumble and fell onto the grass, waving her legs in the air like an upturned beetle while her son poked at her tummy. 'Oh no, the tickle monster has been defeated!' she cried in a mock-heroic tone, before suddenly 'reanimating' with wiggling fingers that sent the boy shrieking across the lawn. She stayed on the ground, grinning at the sky and kicking her heels in the dirt.

topic: A parent watching their child play
text: I watched him try to balance on the low garden wall, so I stepped up behind him and mimicked a tightrope walker’s exaggerated wobble, arms flailing wildly in the air. We moved in a synchronized, silly parade, and I began to narrate our 'death-defying' feat to the neighborhood squirrels in a hushed, announcers' whisper. I hopped down and did a gymnastic finish, arms thrust high in a 'V' shape, waiting for him to do the same so I could hoist him onto my shoulders and run in a dizzying circle until the backyard blurred into green streaks.

topic: A soldier returning home
text: I crept up the driveway with the stealth of a jungle cat, keeping low behind the hydrangea bushes. Seeing the living room window open, I didn't reach for the doorbell; instead, I folded a crisp paper airplane out of a discarded flyer and flicked it through the screen. It spiraled perfectly, landing right in my brother’s lap. As he jumped up in confusion, I popped my head over the sill, wiggling my eyebrows and making a series of rapid, silent hand signals that suggested a top-secret snacks-and-video-games extraction was now in progress.

topic: A soldier returning home
text: The moment the front door creaked open, Elias didn't say a word; he simply dropped his duffel bag and froze in a dramatic 'statue' pose right in the foyer. When his daughter rounded the corner, he began to move in stiff, jerky motions like a wind-up robot, making beeping noises with every step. He chased her toward the kitchen in slow-motion, his fingers wiggling in a 'mechanical' tickle-grip. Even as his parents rushed in, he stayed in character, saluting a kitchen chair with a wink and a metallic 'Affirmative, civilian!'

topic: A soldier returning home
text: I stood on the porch and decided the doorbell was far too mundane for a homecoming. I pulled a small harmonica from my pocket and blew a jaunty, off-key fanfare that sounded more like a kazoo than a musical instrument. When the door swung open, I did a quick, clumsy shuffle-and-tap dance across the welcome mat, tipping an invisible hat to my wife. I caught her in a bear hug and spun her around until we both stumbled against the wall, laughing so hard I could barely stand up straight.

topic: A soldier returning home
text: Sarah hid behind the large oak tree in the front yard, watching her husband pull the groceries from the car. She waited until his arms were full before tossing a small tennis ball toward his feet, then darted to the next tree like she was back in basic training. She let out a soft, mischievous whistle, and as he looked around, she jumped out with a giant, toothy grin and began a series of exaggerated 'interpretive dance' lunges across the lawn. She finished with a jazz-hand flourish, waiting for him to drop the bread in surprise.

topic: A soldier returning home
text: I didn't knock; I just slid a handwritten 'Official Eviction Notice from the Hug Brigade' under the door and waited. When I heard the frantic footsteps on the other side, I crouched down low. The door flew open, and I lunged upward like a jack-in-the-box, letting out a loud, comical 'Boing!' I scooped my little brother up and gave him a 'noogie' while making motorcycle engine noises against his hair. I threw my head back and grinned at the ceiling, my feet doing a little celebratory jig on the hardwood floor.

topic: An artist finishing a painting
text: I dipped the tip of my smallest brush into a glob of neon pink—a color that had absolutely no business being in a moody landscape. With a mischievous glint, I tucked a tiny, barely visible flamingo into the reeds of the Victorian-style marsh. I stepped back, twirling the brush like a baton between my fingers, and stuck my tongue out at the canvas. The serious art critics would spend hours analyzing the 'symbolism' of the hidden pink speck, and the thought made me do a quick, light-footed pirouette in the middle of the paint-splattered studio.

topic: An artist finishing a painting
text: Julian signed his name in the corner with an unnecessarily dramatic flourish, ending the last letter with a curly-cue that resembled a pig's tail. He stood back and began to applaud himself, bowing deeply to a row of empty easels and a very unimpressed-looking cat. 'Magnificent! Encore!' he whispered in a booming theatrical baritone. He picked up a smudge of cerulean blue with his thumb and booped the tip of his own nose, grinning at his colorful reflection in the window before doing a celebratory moonwalk toward the drying rack.

topic: An artist finishing a painting
text: The portrait was done, but the subject looked a bit too stiff for my liking. I took a clean, dry brush and 'tickled' the painted figure's ribs, making a soft 'coochie-coochie-coo' sound under my breath. I felt a surge of energy that made it impossible to stay still, so I used my palette as a makeshift shield and my mahlstick as a sword, engaging in a brief, heroic duel with an imaginary dragon in the corner. I landed a final 'blow' against a stack of sketchbooks and let out a triumphant, stifled snort of laughter.

topic: An artist finishing a painting
text: Sophie balanced her beret at a jaunty, almost vertical angle on her head and began to narrate her own progress in the style of a frantic sports commentator. 'And she goes for the highlight! It’s a bold move! The crowd is silent!' She dabbed a final glint of light onto the painted silver bowl and threw her arms up in a 'V' for victory. She did a little shimmy, her hips swaying to the rhythmic beat of the rain on the skylight, and then spent three minutes trying to balance her palette on top of her head while humming a circus theme.

topic: An artist finishing a painting
text: I looked at the finished mural and decided it needed one final touch: a hidden smiley face drawn in clear varnish that would only appear when the light hit it just right. I chuckled, imagining the owner's surprise months from now. My feet felt light, almost bouncy, as I cleaned my brushes. I began to juggle three dried-out sponges, my eyes wide with focus as I saw how many rotations I could get before one hit the floor. When I finally dropped one, I stuck my hands in my pockets and skipped a full circle around the room, whistling a bright, off-key tune.

topic: A driver stuck in traffic
text: I looked over at the woman in the silver SUV next to me and, once we made eye contact, I began a passionate, silent opera performance, clutching my chest and gesturing wildly to the rhythm of the radio. When she laughed, I took a dramatic bow over the steering wheel, nearly honking the horn with my forehead. I then turned my attention to the rearview mirror, spending the next three minutes seeing how many different ways I could wiggle my ears without moving the rest of my face. The gridlock was just a captive audience for my one-man variety show.

topic: A driver stuck in traffic
text: The sea of brake lights was the perfect opportunity for Leo to practice his finger-tutting. He moved his hands in complex, geometric patterns against the dashboard, imagining he was controlling the movements of the cars ahead with invisible strings. He leaned out the window and let out a series of short, rhythmic whistles, trying to get a bird on a nearby light pole to tweet back in the same key. When the car inched forward three feet, he did a seated 'victory shimmy' and gave the construction cone on the shoulder a jaunty salute.

topic: A driver stuck in traffic
text: I decided to turn the bumper-to-bumper crawl into a game of 'Is that driver a secret spy?' I scrutinized the man in the sedan to my left, narrating his 'mission' in a hushed, cinematic whisper. 'Agent 42 is clearly transporting top-secret sourdough bread,' I muttered, narrowing my eyes with mock suspicion. I reached into the glove box, pulled out a pair of oversized sunglasses, and slid them down my nose with a smirk. I gave him a subtle, two-finger salute, watching with delight as he did a double-take while I winked through the tinted glass.

topic: A driver stuck in traffic
text: Sophie turned the steering wheel into a makeshift drum set, using her thumbs to create a heavy bass line while her rings clattered against the rim like high-hats. She began to improvise a rap about the guy in the truck ahead who was picking his nose, keeping a steady flow until she accidentally made herself snort with laughter. She checked her reflection in the visor mirror and spent a moment trying to see if she could cross only her left eye. Finding success, she held the pose and waited for the passenger in the bus next to her to notice, her shoulders bobbing with suppressed mirth.

topic: A driver stuck in traffic
text: I had managed to balance a spare nickel on its edge atop the gear shift, holding my breath as the car vibrated. Every time the traffic moved an inch, I tried to keep the coin upright with the focus of a zen master. When it finally toppled, I didn't mind; I simply started a new game, trying to see if I could mirror the exact head movements of the bobblehead on the dashboard of the car in front of me. I caught the driver's eye in his mirror and gave him a bright, goofy grin, sticking my tongue out just as the lane finally began to flow.

topic: A doctor delivering news to a patient
text: I didn't just walk into the exam room; I gave the door a rhythmic 'shave and a haircut' knock before sliding in with a grin. Holding the folder behind my back, I looked at the young boy on the table and wiggled my eyebrows. 'Well, the lab results are in,' I said, dropping my voice to a conspiratorial whisper, 'and it turns out you aren't turning into a werewolf after all. Just a regular kid who’s officially cleared for soccer.' I pulled a sticker from my pocket and flicked it toward him like a playing card, watching him catch it with a beam.

topic: A doctor delivering news to a patient
text: Dr. Aris sat on the rolling stool and scooted toward the elderly couple, using his feet to propel himself in a tiny, circular lap before coming to a stop. He tapped the bridge of his nose and pointed at the husband's chart with a wink. 'The news is so good I should probably charge you double for the smile I'm about to give you,' he joked, leaning in close. He mimed a drumroll on his knees and announced the clear scans with a theatrical flourish of his hands, his eyes crinkling behind his spectacles as he watched their tension melt into a shared chuckle.

topic: A doctor delivering news to a patient
text: I checked the vitals and then did a quick, mock-serious frown, looking over the top of my clipboard at my patient. 'I have some very difficult news,' I began, pausing just long enough to see her breath hitch before I broke into a smirk. 'You are officially so healthy that you’re forbidden from coming back to see me for at least six months. I know, it’s a tragedy.' I did a little seated jig on my stool and gave her a high-five, my shoulders bouncing as I told her she was free to go home and celebrate with a very large slice of cake.

topic: A doctor delivering news to a patient
text: Sophie entered the room with a stethoscope draped over her head like a pair of alien antennae, earning an immediate giggle from the patient. She kept the straightest of faces as she checked the charts, then looked up with a bright spark in her eyes. 'The good news is, your broken wing is officially mended,' she declared, miming a bird flapping its wings. She grabbed a tongue depressor and turned it into a makeshift magic wand, tapping the patient’s cast-free arm with a 'ping!' sound effect. 'Go forth and cause trouble,' she whispered with a mischievous grin.

topic: A doctor delivering news to a patient
text: I leaned against the counter in the recovery room and tossed a stress ball into the air, catching it behind my back. 'Hey champ,' I said to the teenager, 'I checked the scans twice. I was looking for signs of a bionic brain, but I guess we'll have to settle for a perfectly normal, healed one.' I mimed a dramatic 'aw shucks' gesture and then tossed the ball to him. As he grinned, I did a quick finger-gun salute and promised him that if he stayed out of trouble, I wouldn't have to use my secret 'no-video-games' prescription power on him.

topic: A musician performing on stage
text: I caught the bassist’s eye during the bridge and stuck my tongue out, launching into a sudden, improvised slide that wasn't in the charts. The crowd roared as I wandered to the edge of the stage, mimicking the front-row fans' frantic dance moves with a cheeky wiggle of my shoulders. I plucked a guitar pick from my pocket and flicked it toward a teenager in a neon hat, winking as they scrambled for it. My fingers danced over the frets with a light, bouncy energy that made the instrument feel more like a toy than a tool.

topic: A musician performing on stage
text: Julian took the final notes of the sonata and turned them into a series of staccato chirps, glancing at the front row with a conspiratorial grin. During the applause, he tucked the violin under his arm like a loaf of bread and did a quick, jaunty shuffle across the hardwood floor. He adjusted his bow as if it were a pool cue, taking a mock 'shot' at the grand piano, which earned a ripple of laughter from the audience. He didn't just bow; he performed a deep, theatrical sweep of his arm that nearly sent his sheet music flying.

topic: A musician performing on stage
text: I hit the high note and followed it up with a rhythmic, mouth-popping sound that sent the drummer into a fit of silent laughter. Between verses, I leaned into the microphone to whisper a secret joke to the front row, then skipped back toward the amplifiers with a spring in my step. I used my tambourine as a makeshift hat, balancing it precariously on my head while I did a slow-motion spin. The air felt charged with a bright, bubbly electricity, and I found myself wanting to turn the entire setlist into a game of musical tag with the band.

topic: A musician performing on stage
text: The cellist didn't wait for the conductor's cue to start the encore; she plucked a bright, twanging note that sounded remarkably like a 'boing' and winked at the first violinist. She swayed so rhythmically with the music that her ponytail whipped around like a golden lasso, and she spent the slower movement making funny, exaggerated 'serious musician' faces at the percussionist. When a stray balloon drifted onto the stage, she nudged it back toward the audience with the scroll of her cello, her eyes sparkling with a mischievous, bright-eyed delight.

topic: A musician performing on stage
text: I sat at the keys and started the intro, but halfway through, I shifted the melody into a bright, ragtime version of a popular cartoon theme. The audience's surprise was met with my wide, toothy grin as I began to play the keys with my elbows during the crescendo. I stood up on the stool for the final chord, throwing my hands in the air with a triumphant 'ta-da!' flourish. I hopped down with the grace of a gymnast and did a series of finger-gun salutes to the balcony, my chest heaving with a buoyant, lighthearted energy.

topic: A traveler arriving in a new city
text: As the train hissed to a stop, I didn't just walk onto the platform; I hopped over the threshold with a silent 'ta-da!' and a wide grin. The foreign architecture looked like a giant gingerbread village, and I found myself stepping only on the blue cobblestones as I navigated toward the exit. I caught my reflection in a shop window and adjusted my backpack straps like a parachute harness, giving myself a sharp, two-finger salute. I pulled out my map, folded it into a makeshift pirate hat for a second, and then began a jaunty, rhythmic march toward the city center, humming a theme song for my own grand adventure.

topic: A traveler arriving in a new city
text: Elias stepped out of the taxi and immediately began a slow, 360-degree spin, his arms outstretched as if he were trying to embrace the towering skyscrapers. He spotted a bronze statue in the plaza and hurried over to mimic its heroic pose, much to the amusement of a group of passing pigeons. When he checked his phone for directions, he decided to walk in time with the blinking pedestrian signal, adding a little extra spring to every fourth step. He winked at a street performer and tossed a coin into the hat with a theatrical flick of his wrist, his eyes darting around with the bright, kinetic energy of a kid in a toy store.

topic: A traveler arriving in a new city
text: I stood at the top of the subway stairs and surveyed the bustling square, feeling like I had just been dropped into a giant, colorful puzzle. Instead of looking for my hotel, I followed a stray balloon for two blocks, dodging through the crowd with the agility of a tag player. I found a fountain and spent five minutes trying to toss a penny so that it would land exactly on a stone gargoyle’s nose. When it finally stuck, I did a quiet victory shimmy and a low bow to a very confused tourist, my shoulders bouncing with a suppressed chuckle as I skipped toward a cafe that smelled like burnt sugar and magic.

topic: A traveler arriving in a new city
text: The air in the new terminal smelled of jet fuel and expensive perfume, and Sarah moved through it with a light-footed grace, occasionally gliding her suitcase along the smooth linoleum like a professional curler. She reached the 'Arrivals' gate and made a series of funny faces at the security cameras, imagining a bored guard in a back room somewhere cracking a smile. She pulled a bright red beret from her bag and plopped it on her head at a jaunty, gravity-defying angle. Spotting a line of luggage carts, she gave one a little shove and rode the back bar for a few glorious, rolling feet, her hair whipping behind her as she stifled a snort of delight.

topic: A traveler arriving in a new city
text: I stepped off the bus and immediately started a mental game of 'I Spy' with the neon signs hanging over the narrow alleyways. 'I spy something... that looks like a giant glowing squid,' I whispered to my backpack, pointing at a convoluted noodle shop sign. I found a line of street tiles that looked like a piano and proceeded to 'play' a silent, hopping version of a nursery rhyme as I walked. Every time a local looked at me with curiosity, I gave them a bright, conspiratorial wink as if we were both in on a very excellent secret. My feet felt light, barely touching the ground as I drifted toward the sound of a distant accordion.

topic: A shopkeeper closing for the day
text: I flipped the 'Open' sign to 'Closed' and performed a quick, silent tap-dance on the threshold before sliding the bolt home. The shop was empty, which meant the center aisle was now my private bowling lane; I took a round orange from the display and rolled it with professional focus toward a cluster of empty milk bottles at the far end. As they clattered over, I threw my arms up in a 'V' and gave a theatrical bow to the rows of canned peas. I hummed a jaunty circus tune while I used the broom as a makeshift guitar, sliding across the freshly waxed floor in my socks.

topic: A shopkeeper closing for the day
text: Arthur wound up the various tin toys on the shelf one by one, watching with a wide grin as a mechanical monkey and a hopping frog raced toward the edge of the counter. He cheered them on in a hushed, frantic sports-announcer voice, leaning in close to see who would claim the 'Grand Prix of the General Store.' When the frog won by a nose, he awarded it a small crown made from a twisted paperclip. He then spent five minutes arranging the mannequins in the window to look like they were in the middle of a high-stakes game of hide-and-seek, winking at their frozen faces before dousing the lights.

topic: A shopkeeper closing for the day
text: I grabbed the heavy iron keys and began to jingle them like a set of sleigh bells, skipping from the register to the back door. Seeing my reflection in the deli case, I paused to practice a series of increasingly ridiculous 'menacing villain' faces, complete with a twirled imaginary mustache and a silent, shoulder-shaking 'mwahahaha.' I decided the floor mats needed a thorough inspection, which I conducted by hopping over them in a game of imaginary lava. I reached the light switch and paused, counting down from three with the dramatic tension of a rocket launch before plunging the room into darkness with a triumphant 'blast off!'

topic: A shopkeeper closing for the day
text: Sophie pulled the heavy security grate down with a rhythmic 'clack-clack-clack,' timing the movement to the beat of the pop song stuck in her head. Once the world was locked out, she used the pricing gun to label her own sleeve as 'Priceless' before aiming it at the shop cat and making a sci-fi laser sound. She did a graceful pirouette in front of the candy counter, snagging a single licorice whip and using it as a conductor's baton to lead an invisible choir of soda bottles. She walked toward the exit on her tiptoes, making exaggerated 'sneaky' gestures to the empty aisles.

topic: A shopkeeper closing for the day
text: I finished the final tally of the till and, finding it balanced perfectly, I gave the cash drawer a friendly 'high-five' as I pushed it shut. I had five minutes before the bus arrived, so I took a handful of packing peanuts and began to juggle them, laughing as the static caused them to stick to my wool sweater like tiny white barnacles. I marched to the front door with the stiff-legged gait of a toy soldier, pausing to balance the ledger on my head for three glorious, wobbly steps. I clicked my heels together, blew a kiss to the silent shelves, and slipped out into the evening air with a bright, mischievous whistle.
